blocks.content: It happened on a Thursday. Not a big thing. Not a decision, exactly.

Eli had pulled Kit to the lunch line early because he had a theory about the pizza, and by the time they got to the table Brooke was already there with two other girls from 7C, and the table was full in a different configuration than usual, and Daisy came in late from a class on the other side of the building and ended up at the far end with Savannah.
Nobody planned it. It was just logistics.

Kit knew where Daisy was sitting because he'd looked. He hadn't meant to look — it was just that when she wasn't in the usual spot his brain went looking for her on its own, which was apparently something it did now.
Brooke said something funny and he laughed because it was funny, and then he looked down the table and Daisy was not looking at him.
At the far end of the table, Savannah leaned close to Daisy.
Savannah looked at the far end of the table where Kit was sitting and then back at Daisy, and didn't say anything else, because Savannah was good at knowing when not to say anything else.
